Explanation: None of the search results provide direct evidence of the age at which Lee Hsien Loong (the prominent man from Singapore who gave Joseph Schooling a cash reward for his 2016 Olympic gold) earned his high school diploma. However, it is well-documented in external sources that Lee Hsien Loong attended National Junior College in Singapore, which typically enrolls students aged 17–18 for A-levels. Lee was born in February 1952 and reportedly graduated from National Junior College in 1970, making him 18 at graduation. This is consistent with the standard Singaporean education timeline.
Exact Answer: 18
Confidence: 90%
